commands.repair_reason: A narrow repair can preserve the useful fallback while rebasing it onto current main, restoring mergeability, adding tests, and adding the required changelog entry.; Needs attention: No dependency or supply-chain changes were found, but the stale session wrapper would drop current route-safety protections if carried forward unchanged.; Review finding: [P2] Rebase before replacing the session wrapper — src/channels/s... (sha=bc8962e5fbee4b2858e0703745b3e9625322b763)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a fallback in `recordInboundSession` to persist delivery context from inbound message metadata when callers omit `updateLastRoute`, plus regression tests for originating and provider/to context.

Reproducibility: yes. for the core missing route persistence: current Mattermost group/channel inbound sets `OriginatingTo` but omits `updateLastRoute`, and current `recordInboundSession` returns before route persistence. No live Mattermost reload plus LLM interruption run was performed for the duplicate flush symptom.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can preserve the useful fallback while rebasing it onto current main, restoring mergeability, adding tests, and adding the required changelog entry.

target.title: fix(session): persist delivery context for inbound non-direct sessions
target.author: beandon13
